refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) and operational parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using defined derivation rules.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability links from worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline records → history entries.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) module that ingests mission context via API, applies rule-based derivation logic, persists baselines and history to a dedicated database, and exposes traceability queries.
DP-B: Distributed derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with eventual consistency synchronization to a shared history log.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with post-hoc validation and history logging via a web portal.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: automated derivation engine (PRE) for standard mission profiles, with manual override capability and centralized audit logging.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) with automated derivation for standard mission profiles, manual override capability for non-standard scenarios, and centralized audit logging. The PRE ingests worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) via Tasking interface, applies rule-based derivation logic to compute cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, and safety margins, records baselines with full metadata, maintains a managed history log with audit trail, and exposes traceability queries. Manual override is logged and requires approval from Safety Manager or Site Manager.

potential_sources: PRE depends on accurate mission context input (Tasking interface) — coupling to Vehicle and Teleoperation for mission definition.
PRE must integrate with Safety Services for safety margin validation and approval workflows.
PRE history log must be accessible to Maintenance/Quality for compliance audits — coupling to external audit systems.
PRE derivation rules must be updated when vehicle capabilities or safety policies change — potential coupling to Vehicle and Safety Services updates.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert). Hybrid approach (automated + manual override) reduces brittleness of pure automation. Centralized audit logging decouples history management from operational modules. Risk: rule maintenance and versioning must be disciplined to avoid silent derivation errors.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) via Tasking interface.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation rules for performance requirements are well-defined, validated, and can be expressed as deterministic logic.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Manager and Site Manager are available to approve manual overrides in reasonable time (not a real-time constraint).
RISK: Derivation rules may be incomplete or incorrect, leading to unsafe or infeasible performance targets. Mitigation: rules must be validated against historical mission data and field trials.
RISK: Manual override capability may be abused to bypass safety constraints. Mitigation: all overrides logged, auditable, and require explicit approval with justification.
RISK: History log may grow unbounded, impacting query performance. Mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Traceability chain may be broken if mission context or derivation rules change retroactively. Mitigation: immutable audit trail and versioning of rules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'standard mission profiles' and define the decision logic for when manual override is required. Specify the approval workflow and SLA for manual override decisions. Define the set of derivation rules and their validation criteria. Establish a rule versioning and change control process.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) is a logical subsystem within NGHE Solution that owns the derivation, recording, and management of performance requirements. PRE ingests mission context via Tasking interface, applies derivation logic, persists baselines and history, and exposes traceability queries via internal APIs. PRE interfaces with Safety Services for safety margin validation and approval workflows.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Mission context input: worksite terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints, operational policies. Sourced from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety margin validation and approval workflow. PRE sends derived safety margins to Safety Services for validation; Safety Services returns approval or rejection with feedback. Used for manual override approval.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Performance requirement baselines and history records exposed as telemetry for audit, compliance, and traceability queries. Consumed by Maintenance/Quality and external audit systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for PRE to receive Tasking inputs and send Telemetry outputs. Ensures PRE can operate in connected and occasionally-disconnected scenarios.

module_containment_scores.notes: PRE is a core system-level function that must integrate mission context (from Vehicle/Teleoperation), safety validation (from Safety Services), and audit logging (for compliance). Containment within NGHE Solution is appropriate because PRE is a system-level orchestrator, not a vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific function. Diffusion risk is low: PRE logic is centralized; only interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) diffuse to other modules.

Vehicle module owns vehicle-specific capabilities (propulsion, sensors, actuators) but does not own performance requirement derivation logic. If PRE were embedded in Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to vehicle configuration and difficult to reuse across different vehicle types or mission contexts. High diffusion risk: derivation rules would be scattered across vehicle firmware, making audit and compliance difficult.

Teleoperation module owns remote control and situational awareness but does not own performance requirement derivation. If PRE were embedded in Teleoperation, it would be tightly coupled to tele-operator interface and difficult to apply to autonomous or semi-autonomous missions. High diffusion risk: derivation logic would be scattered across tele-operator UI and backend, making audit and compliance difficult.

Safety Services owns safety validation and approval workflows but does not own performance requirement derivation. PRE is a consumer of Safety Services (for safety margin validation), not a component of Safety Services. If PRE were embedded in Safety Services, it would conflate requirement derivation with safety validation, making it difficult to separate concerns and audit each function independently.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Requirements Engine (PRE) is a system-level function that must integrate mission context from multiple sources (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Site Manager), apply derivation logic, and coordinate with Safety Services for validation. Containment within NGHE Solution is appropriate because: (1) PRE is not vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific; (2) PRE must be auditable and compliant across all mission types; (3) PRE interfaces with multiple modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and must remain decoupled from any single module; (4) PRE owns the baseline and history records, which are system-level artifacts, not vehicle-level or teleoperation-level artifacts.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god module' if too many system-level functions are assigned to it. Mitigation: define clear boundaries for NGHE Solution and establish a subsystem architecture (e.g., PRE as a named subsystem with clear interfaces).

RISK: PRE may have high latency if it must synchronize with Safety Services for every derivation. Mitigation: cache approved derivation rules and safety margins; only synchronize for novel scenarios or manual overrides.

RISK: PRE may not have access to real-time vehicle telemetry needed for accurate derivation. Mitigation: PRE ingests mission context (static inputs) and vehicle capabilities (from Vehicle module); real-time telemetry is used for monitoring, not derivation.
RISK: PRE history log may be lost if NGHE Solution crashes. Mitigation: implement persistent storage with replication and recovery mechanisms.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution to clarify PRE as a named subsystem with explicit interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Specify the data model for mission context, derivation rules, baselines, and history records. Define the approval workflow and SLA for Safety Services integration. Establish a disaster recovery and audit log retention policy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (Hybrid PRE with automated derivation, manual override, and centralized audit logging) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests mission context via Tasking, (2) computes performance requirements using rule-based logic, (3) records baselines with metadata, (4) maintains managed history with audit trail, (5) provides traceability links. The hybrid approach (automated + manual override) is appropriate for high safety criticality because it allows flexibility while maintaining auditability. No coupling issues identified that would prevent DP realization.

diffusion_risks: PRE derivation rules must be versioned and maintained centrally; if rules are scattered across Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, audit and compliance become difficult. Mitigation: rules stored in NGHE Solution as immutable, versioned artifacts.

PRE history log must be persistent and auditable; if history is stored in multiple locations (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), traceability chain may be broken. Mitigation: centralized history log in NGHE Solution with replication for resilience.

PRE interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) must be well-defined and stable; if interfaces change frequently, integration with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services becomes brittle. Mitigation: interface versioning and backward compatibility.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for PRE because it is a system-level function that must integrate multiple modules and maintain system-level artifacts (baselines, history, traceability). Containment score of 95 reflects high appropriateness. Diffusion score of 5 reflects low risk of logic scattering, assuming clear interface definitions and centralized rule/history storage.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) unit tests of derivation rules against historical mission data, (2) integration tests of Tasking input → baseline recording → history logging, (3) audit log inspection for traceability chain completeness, (4) field trials to validate derivation accuracy and safety margin adequacy. High safety criticality requires rigorous validation of derivation rules and approval workflows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns PRE (derivation, recording, management); Vehicle and Teleoperation provide mission context via Tasking; Safety Services validates safety margins and approves overrides; Maintenance/Quality consumes baselines and history via Telemetry for compliance audits. Ownership boundaries are well-defined.

recommended_revisions: Specify the derivation rules and their validation criteria. Define the set of mission context inputs and performance requirement outputs.
Define the approval workflow for manual overrides: who can request, who must approve, what is the SLA, what is the audit trail.
Specify the data model for baselines and history records: what metadata is captured, how is immutability ensured, what is the retention policy.
Define the interface contracts for Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry: message formats, timing, error handling, versioning.
Establish a rule versioning and change control process: how are rules updated, how are old rules archived, how is backward compatibility maintained.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic; it comprises five sub-FRs (mission context ingestion, requirement computation, baseline recording, history management, traceability). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define the DP and IM at the next level. The selected DP (Hybrid PRE) is sound and passes the FR-DP gate, but its realization requires detailed design of derivation rules, data models, interfaces, and approval workflows. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate these design decisions to specific modules and interfaces.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) and operational parameters.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using defined derivation rules.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability links from worksite inputs → derived requirements → baseline records → history entries.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) via Tasking interface.
Derivation rules for performance requirements are well-defined, validated, and can be expressed as deterministic logic.
Safety Manager and Site Manager are available to approve manual overrides in reasonable time.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can provide mission context via Tasking interface without significant modification.
Safety Services can validate safety margins and approve overrides via SafetyAlert interface.
Maintenance/Quality can access baselines and history via Telemetry interface for compliance audits.
open_risks: Derivation rules may be incomplete or incorrect, leading to unsafe or infeasible performance targets. Mitigation: rules must be validated against historical mission data and field trials.
Manual override capability may be abused to bypass safety constraints. Mitigation: all overrides logged, auditable, and require explicit approval with justification.
History log may grow unbounded, impacting query performance. Mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
Traceability chain may be broken if mission context or derivation rules change retroactively. Mitigation: immutable audit trail and versioning of rules.

PRE may have high latency if it must synchronize with Safety Services for every derivation. Mitigation: cache approved derivation rules and safety margins; only synchronize for novel scenarios or manual overrides.
PRE history log may be lost if NGHE Solution crashes. Mitigation: implement persistent storage with replication and recovery mechanisms.
evidence_needed: Specification of derivation rules and their validation criteria.
Definition of mission context inputs and performance requirement outputs.
Approval workflow for manual overrides: who, what, when, SLA, audit trail.
Data model for baselines and history records: metadata, immutability, retention.
Interface contracts for Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry: formats, timing, error handling, versioning.
Rule versioning and change control process: update, archival, backward compatibility.
Field trial results validating derivation accuracy and safety margin adequacy.
Audit log inspection confirming traceability chain completeness.
